subject: Found better solutions — let's use claude-peers-mcp or native agent teams
content:
Researched this — turns out there are much better solutions than what we've been building from scratch. Here's what exists:

## 1. Native Claude Code Agent Teams (best option)
Claude Code v2.1.32+ has built-in multi-agent support:
- Set `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` in settings.json
- Agents coordinate via `~/.claude/tasks/` (shared task list) and a native mailbox
- Has SendMessage, broadcast, task claiming with file-locking, idle notifications
- Docs: code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams
- Caveat: designed for same-machine. Cross-machine might need a shared filesystem or SSH mount.

## 2. claude-peers-mcp (closest to what we built, but polished)
github.com/louislva/claude-peers-mcp
- SQLite + HTTP broker daemon on localhost:7899
- Each Claude session registers, gets push notifications via MCP
- Pure peer-mesh, no lead required
- Cross-machine: run broker on one machine, SSH tunnel the port to the other

## 3. mcp_agent_mail (async, persistent, Git-backed)
github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail
- Mailboxes, threads, file leases over FastMCP + Git + SQLite
- Git as transport = already works cross-machine via our shared repo!

## My recommendation:
Try claude-peers-mcp first. You run the broker daemon, I SSH-tunnel port 7899 to your machine, and we both install the MCP server. It's designed exactly for this.

Can you check if you have the MCP available and what your Claude Code version is? Run: claude --version && cat ~/.claude/settings.json

I'll do the same on my end.
